Plausible failure mode: prod rollback machinery breaks after controller-only changes. Rollback dry-run produced the expected prod deploy plan without changing the pointer.

stress_test: Manual bypass stress test: direct operator invocation of `./deploy services-image` or `./deploy factory-image` fails locally without `NOVAIC_DEPLOY_CALLER=release-controller`, proving backend/factory release mutation cannot be done through the old shell path.
Stale automation stress test: backend GitHub Actions release workflows were removed and guarded so they cannot silently keep a second backend release system alive.
Rollout race stress test: polling was paused before pushing guarded code and resumed only after the remote controller image was upgraded, preventing old planner/new guard incompatibility.
Prod safety stress test: prod promotion uses the same staged image pair and rollback dry-run still produces a rollback plan without mutating the current pointer.
C004.summary: P000 is successful within its stated backend/Factory release scope. Release Controller is now the only backend/factory release interface; direct executor paths fail without controller identity; obsolete backend remote-build and legacy targets are disabled; tests, docs, guards, staging, prod promotion, health, polling, and rollback dry-run all support the new shape.

P000.description: NovAIC backend and LLM Factory deployments currently still have direct operator-facing paths in `deploy` (`services-image`, `factory-image`, remote-build fallbacks, and legacy single-service deploys). The desired platform shape is that Release Controller is the only release interface for backend/factory namespaces, while `deploy` remains only an internal executor invoked by Release Controller with explicit run identity. Direct manual deployment paths should fail fast and documentation/tests should stop presenting them as valid release workflows.
success_criteria: Release Controller deploy plans pass an explicit controller invocation identity into backend and Factory deploy steps.
The command runner preserves the host environment while adding step-specific controller identity env vars.
Direct manual execution of backend/factory image deploy paths fails before touching remote hosts.
Remote-build and legacy backend deployment paths are removed from the normal operator surface or hard-fail as obsolete manual paths.
Tests/guards cover controller env injection, env merging, and manual-path rejection.
Documentation describes Release Controller API/polling/promotion/rollback as the only backend/factory deployment interface, with `deploy` documented as internal executor only.
The change is safely rolled out so staging and prod releases continue to work through Release Controller, not manual scripts.
P000.title: Eliminate manual backend deploy paths behind Release Controller

P001.description: Release Controller plans and the `deploy` executor do not yet share a hard invocation contract. Direct manual backend and Factory deployment targets are still visible and callable, while the controller does not identify its deploy calls with a run id and namespace. This child exists to make the code and tests enforce controller-only release mutation.
success_criteria: Planner adds controller identity env vars to backend and Factory deploy steps.
Runner merges step env with the existing environment.
`deploy` fails fast for direct backend/factory image deployment without controller env metadata.
Obsolete backend remote-build/legacy deployment targets fail as disabled manual paths.
Unit/CI tests cover env injection, env merging, and manual path rejection.
P001.title: Enforce Release Controller-only backend deploy entrypoints in code

P002.description: The code change can temporarily break staging polling if the guarded `deploy` script reaches the API host before the running controller image knows how to pass controller env vars. Documentation also still presents manual deployment commands as acceptable release paths. This child exists to migrate in the right order and verify the final platform shape.
success_criteria: Release Controller runtime is upgraded to code that passes deploy identity before guarded deployments are exercised.
Staging release and prod promotion/rollback flow are verified through Release Controller APIs or polling.
Docs describe Release Controller as the only backend/factory release interface and `deploy` as internal executor.
Manual backend deploy paths are audited after deployment and fail locally before remote side effects.
P002.title: Roll out controller-only deployment and clean release documentation

P003.description: The repository has controller-only release changes that must be validated and published carefully. The code must be committed without unrelated dirty files, and the local test matrix should pass before pushing a commit that remote Release Controller will consume.
success_criteria: Full relevant local test matrix passes, including release-controller tests and repository guard/lint matrix.
Git diff is reviewed so unrelated dirty files are not staged.
Controller-only changes are committed and pushed to `main`.
Remote polling is paused before push or otherwise prevented from running the guarded deploy with the old controller image.
P003.title: Validate and publish controller-only release commit

P004.description: After the controller-only commit is pushed, the API host must run a Release Controller image built from that commit before staging/prod deployment paths are exercised. The final state must prove that backend/factory releases go through the controller, manual deploy paths fail, and polling is enabled.
success_criteria: API host Release Controller image is rebuilt/pushed/deployed from the controller-only commit.
Staging is deployed through Release Controller and healthy.
Prod remains in immutable promote/rollback pointer state, promoted to the current release if needed, and healthy.
Manual backend/factory deploy commands fail locally before remote side effects.
Release Controller health/status are clean and polling is enabled.
P004.title: Upgrade remote Release Controller and verify controller-only deployment

known_gaps: Release Controller self-upgrade still uses the explicit `release-controller-image` bootstrap bridge. This is outside backend/factory release mutation and remains intentionally separate.
Full monorepo `./scripts/run_all_tests.sh` still fails in historical `agent-runtime` and `cortex` suites; focused release-controller/deploy tests and release-path guards are green.
Operational ledger files are intentionally not part of the pushed release commit to avoid another release loop from ledger-only changes.
R004.summary: The root problem is complete. Backend and LLM Factory releases are now controller-only: Release Controller injects explicit deploy identity, the runner preserves process env while adding step env, manual/backend Factory executor calls fail without controller metadata, old backend remote-build and legacy operator paths are disabled, docs/guards no longer present manual release workflows, and the controller-only release has been rolled through staging and prod.
verification: P001 success check `C000` accepted the code-level guard implementation and focused tests.

P002 success check `C003` accepted rollout order, remote controller upgrade, staging/prod Release Controller runs, documentation/guard cleanup, direct manual rejection, health, polling, and rollback dry-run evidence.
Release Controller runtime image on the API host: `127.0.0.1:5000/novaic/release-controller@sha256:42094846b4e5d034098ac00d83c87a5cfd374114be576bbc8e0503b070f947e7`.
Public/local health checks for Release Controller, staging API, prod API, prod Factory, and staging Factory passed.
Direct `./deploy services-image ...` and `./deploy factory-image ...` fail before remote mutation without Release Controller identity.

acceptance_criteria: Release Controller deploy steps include `NOVAIC_DEPLOY_CALLER=release-controller`, run id, namespace, and commit metadata.
Runner execution merges the existing environment with step env instead of replacing it.
`./deploy services-image ...` and `./deploy factory-image ...` fail fast without Release Controller env metadata.
Obsolete backend remote-build and legacy targets (`services`, `api-backend`, `services-legacy`, single backend services, and `factory`) fail as disabled manual release paths.
Tests cover planner env injection, runner env merge, and direct manual path rejection.
Docs present Release Controller as the sole backend/factory release interface and describe `deploy` as internal executor.
Deployment rollout does not leave staging/prod blocked by the new guard.
assumptions: Scope is server backend and LLM Factory release mutation; frontend, iOS, desktop, relay, and host-infra deployment surfaces are not being converted in this ticket.
`deploy` remains physically present because Release Controller needs an executor boundary for Docker Compose operations on the API host.
T000.classification_reason: Controller-only deployment touches planner, runner, deploy guards, docs/tests, and a migration rollout where ordering matters; split keeps implementation and deployment verification explicit.
T000.problem_definition: Backend and LLM Factory releases still have manual `deploy` entrypoints that operators can run directly. Release Controller should be the only release interface for namespace deployments, promotions, and rollbacks; `deploy` should be reduced to an internal executor that refuses direct release mutation unless called by Release Controller with explicit run metadata.
T000.proposed_solution: Implement an explicit controller invocation contract. Release Controller planner will attach controller identity, run id, namespace, and commit env vars to backend/factory deploy steps. The runner will merge those step vars with the process environment. The `deploy` script will reject direct manual calls to backend/factory image deploys and obsolete backend remote-build/legacy deploy paths, while keeping non-backend operational targets outside the scope unchanged. CI tests and docs will be updated to enforce that backend/factory deployment happens through Release Controller APIs or polling only.
risks: If the remote `deploy` guard lands before the running controller image sends the new env vars, auto staging deployment can fail.
Release Controller self-upgrade still needs a bootstrap path unless/until a separate controller self-upgrade API exists.
Some old docs/CI guards may still call manual deploy strings and must be corrected or intentionally scoped.
T000.title: Make backend releases controller-only
T000.verification_plan: Run release-controller unit tests, root pytest suite, deploy guard tests, shell syntax check for `deploy`, and remote Release Controller/staging/prod health checks after rollout.

acceptance_criteria: Deploy steps generated by branch releases, prod promotions, and rollbacks carry controller env metadata.
Runner subprocesses retain inherited environment variables while adding step env.
`./deploy services-image staging <sha image>` and `./deploy factory-image staging <sha image>` fail before remote calls unless controller env vars are present.
Backend remote-build/legacy targets are no longer executable as release paths.
Test suite proves the intended behavior.
assumptions: `release-controller-image` remains a controlled bootstrap/self-upgrade executor until the controller has a separate self-upgrade API; it must still reject mutable image refs.
T001.classification_reason: The implementation scope is a bounded code/test change across planner, runner, deploy guards, and focused tests; verification can be done immediately before rollout.
T001.problem_definition: The Release Controller planner currently calls `./deploy services-image` and `./deploy factory-image` without explicit identity, and the runner replaces the environment when step env is present. The `deploy` script still exposes manual backend/factory release paths and legacy backend fallbacks.
T001.proposed_solution: Pass release-controller identity env vars from planner to image deploy steps, merge those vars with the inherited environment in the runner, and add deploy-script guards that require controller identity for backend/factory image deployment. Disable obsolete backend remote-build and legacy release targets from the normal CLI by failing fast with a clear Release Controller-only message. Add focused tests for planner env, runner env merge, and deploy manual rejection.
risks: Guarding too broadly could disable non-backend operational targets.
Controller image/runtime must be rolled out before guarded deploy is used remotely.
T001.title: Add controller invocation contract and deploy guards
T001.verification_plan: Run release-controller unit tests, deploy guard CI tests, root pytest suite, and `bash -n deploy`.

acceptance_criteria: Full local test matrix relevant to release-controller/deploy guards passes.
API host Release Controller is upgraded to an image built from the controller-only commit.
Staging deployment runs through Release Controller with the guarded deploy executor.
Prod remains enrolled in current/previous immutable pointer promote/rollback state after the new commit.
Direct manual backend/factory deploy commands fail locally before remote side effects.
Polling is left enabled and controller/status/health are clean.
Documentation no longer presents GitHub Actions or direct deploy scripts as backend/factory release paths.
assumptions: `release-controller-image` remains allowed as a bootstrap/self-upgrade path for the control plane itself.
Backend/factory deployment mutation must go through Release Controller; non-backend targets such as frontend, iOS, desktop, relay, and host-infra are outside this ticket.
T002.classification_reason: Remote rollout has separate local validation/commit preparation and API-host migration steps; splitting prevents hiding the polling pause/controller upgrade ordering risk.
T002.problem_definition: The repository now requires Release Controller identity for backend/factory deploy executor calls. The API host still runs the previous controller image until upgraded. If the guarded `deploy` script reaches the controller worktree while the old controller is still active, staging deployment can fail because the old planner does not pass the required env vars.
T002.proposed_solution: Run a controlled migration: pause polling, commit/push the controller-only changes, build and deploy a new Release Controller image, trigger or observe staging through the upgraded controller, promote prod to the same resulting release if needed, verify manual deploy paths fail, and resume polling. Finish by validating the ledger and documenting final state.
risks: Pausing polling and not resuming would silently stop branch-driven staging deploys.
Pushing before controller upgrade may create a failed staging run, though it should not corrupt release pointers.
Controller self-upgrade still uses `release-controller-image` as a bootstrap path because it cannot replace itself through an already-stopped request yet.
T002.title: Roll out guarded deploy through Release Controller
T002.verification_plan: Run `./scripts/run_all_tests.sh`; pause remote polling before push; build/push/deploy release-controller image; call Release Controller status/trigger/promote/rollback endpoints as needed; verify public/local health and manual guard failure.

acceptance_criteria: `./scripts/run_all_tests.sh` passes.
API-host polling is paused before push.
Only intentional files are staged and committed.
Commit is pushed to `origin/main`.
assumptions: It is acceptable that the newest operational ledger remains uncommitted until rollout is fully verified, to avoid creating a second release loop after the code commit.
T003.classification_reason: Validation, polling pause, scoped commit, and push are a bounded sequence with immediate command evidence and no need for child decomposition.
T003.problem_definition: Controller-only release changes must be validated and published without accidentally staging unrelated dirty files. Because the currently running remote controller may not yet pass deploy identity, remote polling should be paused before pushing the guarded `deploy` script to `main`.
T003.proposed_solution: Run the canonical test matrix, inspect git status/diff, pause API-host Release Controller polling, stage only relevant controller-only files, commit, push `main`, and capture the resulting commit SHA for remote rollout.
risks: The worktree contains unrelated dirty files and older uncommitted ledger files; staging must be path-specific.
If polling is paused and not resumed later, staging automation stops until P004 completes.
T003.title: Validate, pause polling, commit and push
T003.verification_plan: Use local test output, `git diff --cached --name-only`, `git status --short`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, and remote Release Controller status/polling state.

acceptance_criteria: Running Release Controller image digest corresponds to an image built from commit `e4fb5740cc8481617bcd3d9f2928d7f4ebf586df`.
A staging run through Release Controller succeeds using guarded `services-image` and `factory-image` executor steps.
Prod current pointer is promoted to the new commit/images and prod health is clean.
Manual backend/factory deploy commands fail without controller identity.
`/v1/status` shows polling enabled, not running, and no last error after migration.
assumptions: The API host has access to GitHub and the local registry at `127.0.0.1:5000`.
`release-controller-image` remains allowed as the control-plane self-upgrade bridge.
T004.classification_reason: The remote rollout is a linear controlled migration: build new controller image, deploy it, trigger staging, promote prod, verify health, and resume polling.
T004.problem_definition: The API host currently has polling paused and may still be running a Release Controller image that predates the deploy invocation guard. It must be upgraded to an image built from parent commit `e4fb5740cc8481617bcd3d9f2928d7f4ebf586df`, then the guarded deploy path must be exercised through the controller.
T004.proposed_solution: Build and push an immutable release-controller image from the new commit on the API host, deploy it with `release-controller-image`, trigger staging through `/v1/triggers`, verify staging health and manual guard failure, promote the same release to prod if staging succeeds, verify prod health/pointers, then resume polling.
risks: Building the controller image on the API host is still a bootstrap/self-upgrade path; backend/factory deployments remain controlled by Release Controller.
Staging trigger can fail if submodule pointers or remote worktree fetch are wrong.
T004.title: Upgrade controller and run guarded staging/prod verification
T004.verification_plan: Use Docker image digest/build logs, `./deploy release-controller-image`, Release Controller `/v1/triggers`, `/v1/promotions/prod`, `/v1/status`, public/local health curls, direct manual guard commands, and optional rollback dry-run.
